title: 1 Speciedaler - Christian IV (Bust type IIa)

obverse.description: Crowned armored half-bust of King Christian IV facing right over big ornate rounded cartouche with King’s titles and date on four lines. Below the mintmark is divided by cartouche lobes. The King's bust is within a beaded circle with King's motto outside, along rim, divided by the crown.
obverse.lettering:
REGNA · FIR | MAT : PIETAS

CHRITIANUS·IIII
D:G:DAN:NO V:G R.
EX:D:S:H:S:D:C:O:
L:ET:D: 1646 .
HK (h)
obverse.lettering_translation:
Piety strengthens the realms
King Christian the Fourth, King of Denmark and Norway, the Goths and the Wends, duke of Schleswig, Holstein, Stormarn, and Ditmarsh, count of Oldenburg and Delmenhorst

reverse.description: Big ornate oval shield with Danish arms on a cross with long arms encircled by a ring of 13 round shields with arms of Danish territories; all inside an outer pearled circle. Above, cutting the pearled circle, the Danish crown. Below on the lower arm of cross the pendant of the Order of the Elephant. Legend outside circle.
reverse.lettering_translation: The blessing of the Lord makes riches

comments:
This coin has two main variant, depending on the type of mintmark: Type 1 have both HK initials and halberd mark, while Type 2 have only initials. For type 1 exists many subtypes:

TYPE 1A: (Schou #25) Narrow portrait. Year in cartouche is not in an independent line. Medium size central shield partially surrounded by pearls.
Exemple from Heidelberger Münzhandlung Herbert Grün e.K., Auction 52, Lot 598

TYPE 1B: (Schou missing) Narrow portrait. Year in cartouche is not in an independent line. Big size central shield. Cross horizontal arms seems to be formed by four overlapped lines.
TYPE 1C: (Schou #24) Narrow portrait. Year in cartouche is not in an independent line. Big size central shield. Cross horizontal arms are simple and narrow
TYPE 1D: (Schou #22) Wide reclined portrait. Year in cartouche is on a single line, split in two by a rosette. Big size central shield. Cross horizontal arms seems to be formed by four overlapped lines.
TYPE 1E: (Schou #23) Wide reclined portrait. Year in cartouche is on a single line, split in two by a rosette. Big size central shield. Cross horizontal arms are simple and narrow.
TYPE 1F: (Schou #21) Wide reclined portrait. Year in cartouche is on a single line, split in two by a rosette. Small size central shield. Cross horizontal arms are simple and narrow.

TYPE 2A: (Schou #18) Wide portrait not so clearly reclined. Year in cartouche is on a single line, split in two by a rosette. Big size central shield. Cross horizontal arms seems to be formed by four overlapped lines.
TYPE 2B: (Schou #17) Wide reclined portrait. Year in cartouche is on a single line, split in two by a rosette. Big size central shield. Cross horizontal arms seems to be formed by four overlapped lines.

TYPE 2C: (Schou #19 - 20) Wide reclined portrait. Year in cartouche is on a single line, split in two by a rosette. Big size central shield. Cross horizontal arms are simple and narrow.

related_types.title: 1 Speciedaler - Christian IV (Bust type I; Oval provincial shields)
